hero.tagline: Save 50–70% on Dental Care in Costa Rica
hero.subtitle: World-class implants, orthodontics, and smile makeovers — at a fraction of US and Canadian prices.

about.title: About Dental City
about.p1: Founded by two graduates of the University of Costa Rica (UCR), Dr. Francisco Rodriguez Chaves and Dr. Zaidy Mariela Hernandez Bonilla, Dental City Costa Rica has grown into a trusted name in dental care with over 20 years of clinical experience.
about.p2: With two locations serving Aguas Zarcas and La Virgen de Sarapiqui, we offer a comprehensive range of services from routine care and orthodontics to advanced implant procedures, full-mouth rehabilitation, and in-house 3D diagnostic imaging.
about.p3: Whether you're a local resident or traveling to Costa Rica for affordable, high-quality dental care, our bilingual team is here to make your experience comfortable and stress-free.

services.title: Our Services
services.subtitle: From preventive care to complex restorations, we offer a full spectrum of dental services at a fraction of US prices.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything international patients want to know about dental care in Costa Rica.

items.question: How much can I save compared to US or Canadian prices?
items.answer: Most patients save 50–70% on dental procedures. For example, a dental implant with crown that costs $3,500–$6,000 in the US is $1,200–$1,800 at Dental City. All-on-4 full-arch rehabilitation starts around $7,500 compared to $20,000–$35,000 in the US.

items.question: Do your dentists speak English?
items.answer: Yes! Our patient coordinator Marlon is fluent in English and guides international patients through every step. Our dentists communicate in English as well, so you'll always understand your treatment plan.

items.question: What are your dentists' qualifications?
items.answer: All our dentists are graduates of the University of Costa Rica (UCR), the top-ranked university in Central America. Dr. Rodriguez has 11+ years specializing in orthodontics with 1,500+ cases. Dra. Hernandez has 15+ years in implant dentistry and oral rehabilitation.

items.question: How do I plan a dental trip to Costa Rica?
items.answer: It's simple: (1) Send us your X-rays or photos via WhatsApp, (2) We'll send you a treatment plan and quote within 24 hours, (3) Book your flights and accommodation, (4) We handle the rest. Many patients combine treatment with a vacation!

items.question: How long will my treatment take?
items.answer: It depends on the procedure. Cleanings, fillings, and crowns can often be completed in 1–2 visits. Implants typically require 2 trips spaced 3–4 months apart. Orthodontics requires regular follow-ups. We'll give you a clear timeline before you commit.

items.question: Do you accept US dental insurance?
items.answer: We don't bill insurance directly, but we provide detailed invoices and documentation that many US insurance plans accept for out-of-network reimbursement. Many patients find the savings so significant that even without reimbursement, it's worth it.

items.question: What payment methods do you accept?
items.answer: We accept cash (USD and colones), credit cards (Visa, Mastercard), and bank transfers. We can also create flexible payment plans for larger treatments.

items.question: Is dental care in Costa Rica safe?
items.answer: Absolutely. Costa Rica is one of the top dental tourism destinations worldwide. Our clinic uses the same sterilization protocols, materials, and technology as leading US clinics. We're members of the Colegio de Cirujanos Dentistas de Costa Rica, the national regulatory body.

socialProof.patientQuote: The costs were like a quarter of what I was quoted in the US. Highly recommended!
